revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Amatsuka Kofun]][[ja:天塚古墳]][[zh:天塚古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Amazuka Kofun | image = File:Amazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Ukyō Ward|qid=Q580186|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.007222|N|135.711944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| iso = JP-26 | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | area = Kofun length: 73m | built = Early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} | burial = Two {{ill|Horizontal stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Interred_individual = Presumed {{ill|Hata clan|en|Hata clan|qid=Q987290|lt=Hata clan|1=Hata clan}} family members | burial goods = Numerous grave goods and {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} | Historic Site = National Historic Site of Japan | notable_features = | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto city|coordinates=35|coordinates=0|coordinates=25.57|lon_deg=135|lon_min=42|lon_sec=43.45|map_dot_label=Amazuka Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tumulus.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none|qq=}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Amazuka Kofun''' (天塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q580186|lt=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}}, Matsumoto-cho, Uzumasa, [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]]. It is Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. == Overview == Located at the western edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Basin|1=Kyoto Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10883263}} (Yamashiro Basin), on the southern edge of the Sagano Plateau, Amazuka Kofun is an ancient Kofun built during the early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}}. Excavations have not been conducted, but grave goods were unearthed in 1887{{Sfn|Amazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981|qq=}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the rectangular front section oriented southward. The mound has two tiers{{Sfn|Amazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981|qq=}}{{Sfn|Amazuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989|qq=}}{{Sfn|Kyoto Archaeological Intypeation No. 65|1997|pp=41-42|qq=}}. Circular {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} have been found on the mound’s exterior{{Sfn|Amazuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989|qq=}}, and the mound is surrounded by a moat{{Sfn|Amazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981|qq=}}{{Sfn|Amazuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989|qq=}}. The burial facilities consist of two {{ill|Horizontal stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located in the rounded rear section{{Sfn|Kyoto Archaeological Intypeation No. 65|1997|pp=41-42|qq=}}. A circular tumulus presumed to be an accompanying mound is located to the northwest{{Sfn|Amazuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989|qq=}}. This kofun is part of a cluster of late-period tumuli in the Sagano area, including the {{ill|Hebizuka Kofun|en|Hebizuka Kofun|qid=Q11625508|lt=Hebizuka Kofun|1=Hebizuka Kofun}}, {{ill|Suikizan Kofun|ja|垂箕山古墳|1=Tarumi-yama Kofun|2=ja|3=垂箕山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suikizan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi-yama Kofun|qq=}}, and the now-destroyed Shimizuyama Kofun. These mounds are thought to belong to the {{ill|Hata clan|en|Hata clan|qid=Q987290|lt=Hata clan|1=Hata clan}}, a prominent immigrant lineage that developed the region<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://www.kyoto-arc.or.jp/news/leaflet/190.pdf "Exploring the Tumuli of Uzumasa"]|qq=}} (Kyoto City Archaeological Research Institute, 2004).</ref>. The site was Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978<ref name="NationalHistoric Site"/>. Access to one of the two stone chambers is currently restricted. == History == * 1887: Grave goods were unearthed{{Sfn|Amazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981|qq=}}. * 1900: burial goods were deposited at the Imperial Museum of Kyoto (now the {{ill|Kyoto National Museum|en|Kyoto National Museum|qid=Q147286|lt=Kyoto National Museum|1=Kyoto National Museum}}). A survey was conducted by [[:en:Hamada Kosaku|Hamada Kosaku]]{{Sfn|Kyoto Archaeological Intypeation No. 65|1997|pp=41-42|qq=}}. * Taisho period (1912–1926): Documented by {{ill|Umehara Sueji|ja|梅原末治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umehara Sueji|1=Sueji Umehara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2478238}}{{Sfn|Kyoto Archaeological Intypeation No. 65|1997|pp=41-42|qq=}}. * 1966: Overview report on Amazuka and Shimizuyama Kofun by the Kyoto University Archaeological Research Association{{Sfn|Kyoto Archaeological Intypeation No. 65|1997|pp=41-42|qq=}}. * 1973: The nearby Shimizuyama Kofun was destroyed{{Sfn|Amazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981|qq=}}. * March 3, 1978: Designated a National Historic Site<ref name="NationalHistoric Site"/>. * 1984: Moat fragments and Haniwa pieces were discovered during public sewer construction{{Sfn|Trial Excavation Report in Kyoto City, 1985|1985|qq=}}. == Kofun == The mound area are as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;font-size:85%" ! colspan="2" | ! Japanese Encyclopedia of Kofun{{Sfn|Amazuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989|qq=}}・National Historic Site Guide{{Sfn|National Historic Site Guide|qq=}}・Database<ref name="NationalHistoric Site"/> ! Kyoto City Place names{{Sfn|Amazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981|qq=}}・Kyoto Archaeological Intypeation{{Sfn|Kyoto Archaeological Intypeation No. 65|1997|pp=41-42|qq=}} |- | colspan="2" | Total length | 73m || 71m |- | rowspan="2" | Rounded rear section | Diameter | 40m || 45.5m |- | Height | 8–9m<ref name="NationalHistoric Site"/> || 8m |- | rowspan="2" | Rectangular front section | Width | 50m || 58m |- | Height | 8.5–9m<ref name="NationalHistoric Site"/> || 8.4m |} The mound’s keyhole type reflects late Kofun-period characteristics{{Sfn|Amazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981|qq=}}. Among Sagano's kofun, its length Ranks second after {{ill|Hebizuka Kofun|en|Hebizuka Kofun|qid=Q11625508|lt=Hebizuka Kofun|1=Hebizuka Kofun}} (75m){{Sfn|Kyoto Archaeological Intypeation No. 65|1997|pp=41-42|qq=}}<ref name="NationalHistoric Site"/>. == burial == Two {{ill|Horizontal stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} were constructed in the rounded rear section. One opens to the northwest, while the other opens at the constricted central section. ; Northwest-opening stone chamber : A sleeve-less stone chamber constructed with random stone masonry. The chamber area are: :* Total length: Approx. 10m :* Width: 2m (center), 1.1m (entrance) :* Height: Approx. 2m (center) : An Inari shrine is now enshrined near the back wall{{Sfn|Amazuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989|qq=}}. ; Central-section stone chamber : A one-sleeved stone chamber with the following estimated area: :* Total length: 7.7m :* burial chamber: :** Length: 4.7m :** Width: 1.7m :** Height: Approx. 2m :* Entrance corridor: :** Length: Approx. 3m :** Width: 1.2m :** Height: 1.5m : Like the other chamber, it also has an Inari shrine{{Sfn|Amazuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989|qq=}}. == burial goods == burial goods unearthed in 1887 include: * Beads * Seven hollow gilt-bronze balls * Mirrors * Three iron swords * Iron arrowheads * Horse fittings * {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} (vessels, jars) The items are stored at the {{ill|Kyoto University Museum|en|Kyoto University Museum|qid=Q11374929|lt=Kyoto University Museum|1=Kyoto University Museum}}, though they are not on public display{{Sfn|Kyoto Archaeological Intypeation No. 65|1997|pp=41-42|qq=}}. == Shimizuyama Kofun == Amazuka Kofun's northeast neighbor was the now-destroyed '''Shimizuyama Kofun'''. This keyhole-shaped tumulus, built in the late 5th to early 6th century, measured approximately 60m in length. It was likely a predecessor Kofun for local elites{{Sfn|Amazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981|qq=}}{{Sfn|Kyoto Archaeological Intypeation No. 65|1997|pp=41-42|qq=}}. == Historic Site == === National Historic Sites === * Amazuka Kofun - Designated on March 3, 1978<ref name="NationalHistoric Site">{{NationalHistoric SiteDatabase|401|1739|Amazuka Kofun|qq=}}</ref>. == location == '''location:''' * {{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q580186|lt=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}}, Matsumoto-cho, Uzumasa, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan. '''Access:''' * Railway: ** {{ill|Keifuku Electric Railroad|en|Keifuku Electric Railroad|qid=Q3537126|lt=Keifuku Electric Railroad|1=Keifuku Electric Railroad}} Arashiyama Line {{ill|Kaikonoyashiro Station|en|Kaikonoyashiro Station|qid=Q11625482|lt=Kaikonoyashiro Station|1=Kaikonoyashiro Station}} (6-minute walk south) ** [[Kyoto Municipal Subway]] Tozai Line {{ill|Uzumasa Tenjingawa Station|en|Uzumasa Tenjingawa Station|qid=Q2374167|lt=Uzumasatenjingawa Station|1=Uzumasa Tenjingawa Station}} (8-minute walk southwest) == References == * {{small|Interpretive board installed by Kyoto City|qq=}} * Local government reports and encyclopedias * Archaeological research publications <references />
== See Also == * {{ill|Hata clan|en|Hata clan|qid=Q987290|lt=Hata clan|1=Hata clan}}
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
